sn47.41:1.2: “Monks, remain with your minds well-established in the four establishings of mindfulness.
sn47.41:1.3: Don’t let the deathless be lost for you.
sn47.41:1.5: There is the case where a monk remains focused on the body in & of itself—ardent, alert, & mindful—subduing greed & distress with reference to the world.
sn47.41:1.8: mental qualities in & of themselves—ardent, alert, & mindful—subduing greed & distress with reference to the world.
sn47.41:1.9: “Monks, remain with your minds well-established in these four establishings of mindfulness.
sn47.41:1.10: Don’t let the deathless be lost for you.”
